bio: Mahmoud Ahmadinejad served as mayor of Tehran from 2003 before his presidential election victory in 2005. His two-term presidency was defined by confrontational nuclear rhetoric, Holocaust denial, economic mismanagement, and the disputed 2009 election that triggered the [Green Movement](/events/green-movement-protests-2009). He significantly expanded the [IRGC](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps)'s economic and political power. After leaving office he fell out with Khamenei and was disqualified from subsequent presidential races.
editorial_notes: Still living as of April 2026. Disqualified from 2017 and 2021 presidential elections by Guardian Council.
